title: Own the loop: how designers and researchers win with AI evals
key_points: Argues that the teams winning with AI succeed not by moving fast but by measuring well, and that good measurement begins with a human-centered definition of what 'good' means, owned by design and research.
Reframes AI evaluation, traditionally treated as an engineering problem, as something that should now be led by designers and researchers.
Claims that as AI capabilities commoditize, design becomes the new competitive moat.
Issues an invitation for designers and researchers to become architects of how AI is evaluated against real human needs.
